features.name: Zig 0.17-dev resolved and locked by Pantry
features.notes: The exact compiler build is locked locally and in CI.

features.name: No Git submodules
features.notes: Sibling repositories are locked by commit and checked out directly in CI.

features.name: Allocator, ownership, concurrency, feature, and atomic hot-reload contracts
features.notes: Runtime publication pins in-flight requests to immutable generations; reclamation, errors, feature discovery, and connector responsibilities are explicit and tested.

features.name: Five inspection phases plus logging lifecycle
features.notes: Connection setup is pre-phase; the five stable phase numbers, legal transitions, early exits, terminal drop behavior, and exactly-once logging finalization are enforced.

features.name: Detection-only interventions
features.notes: Interventions preserve evidence, are marked non-enforcing, and do not interrupt or terminate detection-only transactions. Detailed action precedence remains scoped to WAF-15.

features.name: Bounded scalar transaction variables with phase availability and origins
features.notes: The 78-name stable scalar registry, aliases, origins, phase gates, connection/request/response producers, UTC timing, monotonic duration, body diagnostics, identity, persistent RESOURCE binding, matched values, and minimum-severity semantics are executable. Full buffered body materialization remains WAF-24/WAF-28.

features.name: Bounded collections, targets, exclusions, counts, origins, and runtime macros
features.notes: The 37-namespace registry, bounded owned storage, repeated/map mutations, source offsets, phase gates, exact and zig-regex selectors, exclusions, counts, explicit matcher errors, and configured baseline macro semantics are executable. HTTP/body/XML producers and persistent backends remain in their linked downstream issues.

features.name: libinjection SQLi and XSS detection
features.notes: @detectSQLi and @detectXSS use the full libinjection port in zig-injection (a byte-exact translation of libinjection-go v0.3.2 validated against the pinned 54-case SQLi and 118-case folding corpora and the libinjection XSS differential vectors). They pass the retained Coraza detectSQLi and detectXSS corpora. Allocation-free with reusable per-worker scratch.

features.name: Numeric, string, and regex scalar operators
features.notes: eq/ge/gt/le/lt, beginsWith/endsWith/contains/strmatch/within/streq/containsWord, and rx/rxGlobal/rsub resolve case-insensitively to a typed union. Numeric parsing preserves the ModSecurity std::stoi and Coraza strconv.Atoi profiles; rx exposes capture fields 0..9, bounded compile and runtime errors, empty-pattern always-match, and optional per-worker LRU memoization. rsub preserves the pinned ModSecurity 3.0.16 always-true stub. Evidence covers the 118-case Coraza operator corpus and ReleaseFast benchmarks. Engine rule-evaluation wiring and detectSQLi/detectXSS parity remain separate issues.

features.name: Encoding and byte-range validation operators
features.notes: validateByteRange (compiled allowed-byte set with empty-arg always-match), validateUtf8Encoding, and validateUrlEncoding match the pinned Coraza semantics and pass the retained 53-case corpus. GeoIP, RBL, GSB, fuzzy-hash, file-inspection, national-identifier, and schema validation operators remain later slices of WAF-20, several gated on external data or the XML subsystem.

features.name: Phrase (Aho-Corasick) and IP CIDR operators
features.notes: pm/pmFromFile/pmFromDataset compile to an ASCII case-insensitive Aho-Corasick automaton with O(1)-per-byte membership, bounded state counts, empty-pattern always-match, and a non-overlapping capture iterator. ipMatch/ipMatchFromFile/ipMatchFromDataset parse IPv4 and zero-compressed IPv6 CIDR subnets with the pinned bare-address /32 and /128 defaults, unparseable-token skipping, and Go net.To4 IPv4-mapped normalization. Evidence replays the retained Coraza pm (15) and ipMatch (3623) corpora with fuzz and ReleaseFast benchmarks. Dataset registry and reload wiring land with the engine dataset subsystem.

features.name: Query-string argument and cookie parsing
features.notes: processUri parses the query string into ARGS_GET/ARGS with the pinned Coraza doParseQuery split and x-www-form-urlencoded queryUnescape, honoring the configured SecArgumentSeparator; addRequestHeader parses the Cookie header into REQUEST_COOKIES. Focused, engine-integration, and fuzz coverage are executable. Body-argument processors, cookie v0/v1 formats, path arguments, smuggling-safe host parsing, and decoding-error variables remain later slices of WAF-23.

features.name: Bounded request-body buffering and processors
features.notes: The request body is buffered in memory up to the request-body limit and exposed as REQUEST_BODY (honoring SecRequestBodyAccess), with a reject/process-partial policy and a spool-sink boundary for disk spooling. The URLENCODED and JSON body processors populate ARGS_POST (JSON flattened into dotted json.* keys with array-length entries, matching Coraza). Temp-file spooling, multipart (WAF-25), and XML (WAF-27) body processors remain.

features.name: Response-header and body inspection
features.notes: Response headers populate RESPONSE_HEADERS/RESPONSE_CONTENT_TYPE and the response body is buffered and exposed as RESPONSE_BODY (honoring SecResponseBodyAccess). The response JSON body processor is opt-in via ctl:responseBodyProcessor (Coraza does no Content-Type auto-detection for responses) and flattens the body into RESPONSE_ARGS with the same dotted json.* keys and array-length entries as the request side, publishing RES_BODY_PROCESSOR and raising RES_BODY_PROCESSOR_ERROR on invalid JSON. Response-body mutation and content injection remain.

features.name: Revisioned TX and persistent IP, SESSION, USER, GLOBAL, and RESOURCE collections
features.notes: Bounded transaction-local mutation, persistent initialization, expiry-only state, optimistic conflict rebasing, typed fail-open/fail-closed behavior, thread-safe in-memory storage, and Pantry-linked LMDB storage are executable. SecLang text parsing and action-plan wiring consume these APIs in WAF-10 through WAF-15.

features.name: Versioned C connector ABI
features.notes: Opaque ownership, sized structs, lifecycle, feature discovery, and errors are implemented; callbacks and ABI matrix remain.

features.name: Immutable cache-efficient structural execution plans
features.notes: Owned typed IR, phase indexes, defaults, chains, action families, conservative prefilters, immutable macro programs, stable diagnostics, deterministic fingerprints, atomic component sharing, WAF generation pinning, corpus compilation, fuzzing, and ReleaseFast metrics are verified. WAF-12 owns complete directive-specific semantic validation.

features.name: Rule defaults, removals, updates, markers, and authenticated remote rules
features.notes: Phase-scoped defaults, ID/tag/message removal, ordered target and action overlays, source-ordered marker linking, explicit missing-rule policy, and authenticated bounded remote assembly are immutable compile-time behavior. Pinned upstream-derived fixtures, allocation-failure tests, deterministic fuzzing, corpus gates, and baseline/overlay ReleaseFast measurements are executable.

features.name: Typed metadata, non-disruptive effects, captures, persistence, and owned event intent
features.notes: Metadata and effect text compile once; standalone rules and complete chains apply through bounded atomic local/persistent staging. Post-action owned intent, capture replacement, transaction-local ENV, checked TX/persistent arithmetic, binding, expiry, deterministic decay, failure policy, differential fixtures, fuzzing, and latency/allocation benchmarks are executable.

features.name: Typed disruptive decisions, flow cursor, and transaction runtime controls
features.notes: Atomic decisions, allow scopes, skip/skipAfter, transaction-local engine/audit/body controls, exact rule-tag and exact/bounded zig-regex rule-target exclusions, capability-gated proxy, exhaustive allocation rollback, fuzzing, and ReleaseFast decision/cursor/exclusion benchmarks are executable. Pause duration parsing and execution remain explicitly assigned to WAF-21.

features.name: Stable transformation union, aliases, ordered pipelines, and bounded caching
features.notes: All 35 typed kinds, two American-spelling aliases, and the compile-time none reset resolve case-insensitively, execute in published plan order with multiMatch checkpoints and bounded transaction caching, and preserve profile-specific ModSecurity and Coraza byte semantics. Evidence covers the 355-case retained Coraza corpus, per-kind differential vectors, upstream inventory drift, deterministic fuzzing, and ReleaseFast hot-path benchmarks with zero steady-state allocation. Plugin transformation execution remains assigned to WAF-22.

features.name: Pantry-managed PostgreSQL 18 and native libpq 18
features.notes: Packages are locked; per-project cluster/port isolation is still required.

features.name: STX development and embedded production assets
features.notes: The workspace and pinned STX source exist; the fingerprinted asset compiler remains.

features.name: HTTP/1.1 reverse proxy
features.notes: Standalone data-plane implementation has not started.

features.name: HTTP/2 reverse proxy through nghttp2
features.notes: The adapter is scheduled after HTTP/1.1 framing and backpressure.
